result.summary: Sensitive Data Protection on dlp.googleapis.com exposes a Google Cloud REST API and related gcloud alpha dlp CLI commands; both use Google Cloud OAuth access tokens.
result.description: Sensitive Data Protection, formerly Cloud DLP, is a Google Cloud service for discovering, classifying, and de-identifying sensitive data in text, files, databases, and other data sources. It supports inline inspection/transformation as well as discovery and profiling workflows across Google Cloud resources.

gcp_oauth.label: Google Cloud OAuth 2.0 access token
gcp_oauth.setup: Create or use a Google Cloud project in the [Google Cloud console](https://console.cloud.google.com/), enable the DLP API for that project, and obtain an access token using Google Cloud authentication (for example via [Application Default Credentials](https://cloud.google.com/docs/authentication/provide-credentials-adc) or `gcloud auth application-default login` / `gcloud auth print-access-token`). The Sensitive Data Protection auth guide is at [Authenticating to the DLP API](https://docs.cloud.google.com/sensitive-data-protection/docs/auth).
